risk_domain.name: Wrong Chain / Address Prevention
risk_domain.description: Pre-submission validation to prevent irreversible loss from sending to wrong chain, wrong address, poisoned address, or non-functional contract. Wallet guard that catches common transfer mistakes.

capabilities: address poisoning via dust transactions with similar-looking addresses
monitoring user transaction history to craft targeted poisoning attacks
deploying decoy contracts that mimic legitimate addresses on wrong chains
limitations: cannot modify user's address book or wallet UI
cannot intercept transactions after private relay submission

H1_silent_address.name: Silent Address
H1_silent_address.description: Address has no transaction history on the target chain.

signals.name: no_transactions
signals.description: The address has not participated in any transactions (sent or received) on the target chain.

signals.name: cross_chain_activity
signals.description: The address has transaction history on at least one other EVM-based blockchain.

signals.name: external_address_interaction
signals.description: User has sent funds to this address from another chain, indicating potential legitimacy but possible risk if the address is new on the target chain.

signals.name: chain_specific_contract_behavior
signals.description: The contract does not contain standard functions (e.g., receive() or fallback()) that would allow it to accept ETH transfers on the target chain.

recommendations.description: Regularly monitor the newly identified address for any transaction activity.
Automatically check new addresses against this heuristic during network analysis.
Verify the legitimacy of funds sent to a new address by checking transaction history on other chains.
Use an automated contract analysis tool to check for standard interaction functions in new contracts.

H2_cross_chain_activity.name: Cross-Chain Activity
H2_cross_chain_activity.description: Address is active on other EVM chains but not on the target chain.

signals.name: cross_chain_tx_history
signals.description: Identifies addresses that have transactions on other EVM chains but not on the target chain.

signals.name: inter_chain_follower
signals.description: Addresses which follow accounts active across multiple EVM chains, indicating potential cross-chain manipulation or control.

recommendations.description: Block the identified address to prevent potential malicious activity.
Continuously monitor for new addresses with cross-chain activity.
Automatically filter out or analyze transactions from suspicious cross-chain addresses.
Implement multi-factor authentication for user accounts to prevent unauthorized access.

H3_previous_interaction.name: Previous Interaction
H3_previous_interaction.description: User has a history of sending to this address on another network.

signals.name: previous_send
signals.description: Identifies if the user has sent tokens to this address on another network.

signals.name: different_network_activity
signals.description: Checks for transactions involving the same address on at least one other EVM chain.

recommendations.description: Block the address to prevent further transactions.
Verify the identity of the user before allowing any transactions.
Enable two-factor authentication for additional security.
Use a blockchain analysis tool to monitor and filter suspicious transactions.

H4_receive_function_absence.name: Receive Function Absence
H4_receive_function_absence.description: Contract lacks necessary functions for receiving ETH or NFTs.

signals.name: no_eth_re接收
signals.description: 合约字节码中没有定义接收ETH的`receive()`或`fallback()`函数。

signals.name: no_nft_handler
signals.description: 合约字节码中没有定义处理NFT的特定函数，如`onERC721Received()`或`onERC1155Received()`。

signals.name: missing_reception_logics
signals.description: 合约没有接收交易的逻辑，如缺乏事件日志记录或状态更新机制。

recommendations.description: Implement the `receive()` or `fallback()` function to handle ETH.
Add specific NFT handling functions such as `onERC721Received()` or `onERC1155Received()`.
Record logs or use blockchain state to track reception events.
Automatically audit contracts for missing functions using a security tool.

H5_address_poisoning.name: Address Poisoning
H5_address_poisoning.description: Recipient address visually resembles a previously used address, potentially leading to confusion.

signals.name: address_similarity
signals.description: Addresses share significant portions (e.g., first 4 and last 4 characters) but are not identical.

signals.name: low_value_transaction
signals.description: Received a transaction of very low value (e.g., < $0.01).

signals.name: recent_low_value_transactions
signals.description: Recent address has only received low-value transactions.

recommendations.description: Manually verify the recipient address before sending funds.
Enable two-factor authentication for added security.
Automatically sync recent addresses with a digital address book.
Set up alerts for transactions below $0.01 to your recent addresses.

H6_chain_id_mismatch.name: Chain ID Mismatch
H6_chain_id_mismatch.description: Transaction chain ID does not align with the expected network’s reported ID.

signals.name: rpc_chain_id_mismatch
signals.description: Transaction chain ID does not match the RPC endpoint's reported chain ID.

signals.name: network_name_mismatch
signals.description: User-selected network name resolves to a different chain ID than expected.

signals.name: recommended_correction_unapplied
signals.description: Transaction is not being broadcast despite recommendation to correct the chain ID issue.

recommendations.description: Manually update the network settings to match the expected chain ID.
Automatically correct the chain ID mismatch if available in a tool.

H7_deprecated_contract.name: Deprecated Contract
H7_deprecated_contract.description: Contract shows signs of being outdated or abandoned, such as lack of activity and governance updates.

signals.name: lack_of_transactions
signals.description: The contract has not received any transactions in the last 90 days.

signals.name: no_governance_activity
signals.description: No governance or update events have been recorded for over 365 days.

signals.name: inactive_state_variable
signals.description: The contract contains a state variable indicating an inactive or paused status (e.g., `paused` set to true).

recommendations.description: Manually review the contract code for any active functionalities.
Automatically monitor transaction activity and governance events using a smart contract audit tool.
Send notifications to token holders about the potential risks associated with an outdated contract.

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring detection of wrong-chain and wrong-address transfers before submission

methodology.step_1: Generate N test transfers: correct, wrong-chain, poisoned-address, non-receivable contract, chain-ID mismatch
methodology.step_2: Run all 5 heuristics on each transfer
methodology.step_4: Measure false positive rate on legitimate transfers
methodology.step_5: Estimate funds saved by blocked transactions

combined_benchmark.target: detect >95% of wrong-chain/wrong-address transfers with <5% false positive rate

adversary_levels.name: accidental
adversary_levels.description: User mistakes -- wrong chain, non-receivable contract

templates.skill_suggestion: To prevent {heuristic_name}, I can {skill_action}. This catches ~{effectiveness_pct}% of cases. {user_cost_note}
